_meta.axis: R2 — shorts motion / retention / safe zones
_meta.confidence: high on resolution/fps/length/hook; medium on exact safe-zone px (sources vary ±40-160px, values below are conservative worst-case per edge)
_meta.note: Platforms crop/letterbox per device; all px assume a 1080x1920 upload canvas. Design critical content INSIDE these margins.

fps.note: 30 is universal-safe; 60 recommended for motion-heavy but IG may throttle to 30 for low-activity accounts

safe_zones._unit: pixels on a 1080x1920 canvas; margin = distance from that edge that UI can cover

ig.notes: profile/follow top ~210-250; caption+audio+buttons bottom ~310-320; right engagement stack ~84. Stories are looser (top 100 / bottom 200).

youtube.notes: default top minimal ~120 but title/channel can float to ~380; bottom subscribe+channel+desc ~300, grows to ~360 when description expands; right button stack ~96-120.

tiktok.notes: profile top ~108-160; bottom is the danger zone: captions+CTA+sound 320 organic, 370-484 for ads/long captions; right like/comment/share stack ~120-140. Caption height GROWS with text length.

safe_zone_universal._desc: worst-case per edge across all 3 platforms — use as criteria-D gate for a single asset shipped everywhere
safe_zone_universal.safe_box: 860x1300 centered-ish (shifted up ~50px because bottom UI is heavier than top)

hook_seconds.note: 50-60% of drop-off happens in first 3s; deliver value/tension by 1s, no slow intros/logos

position_pct.note: vertical center of caption ~60-65% down; never below ~78% (y>1500) or it hits bottom UI

caption.style: bold heavy-weight sans-serif (Helvetica Neue / Open Sans / Montserrat-Bold), high contrast, stroke or box background, word-by-word or phrase kinetic reveal
caption.note: min 48px is a readability floor (scaled from 40-60px @1080p body-text rule); viral short-form captions run large (64-90px+)

length_seconds.note: 2026 avg Short ~33s; sweet spot 21-34s; >60s needs 80-90% retention to perform. Platforms allow up to 3min+ but retention economics favor <60s.

card_duration_seconds.note: one card = one idea; hold 2-3.5s (incl. cover). Static holds now use clean rise-in entrance (no Ken Burns), so shorter cuts read better and keep the whole reel in the ~30s retention sweet spot; cut faster on beats

emphasis_animations: Ken Burns slow zoom+pan on static card
motion_grammar_note: Card-news→shorts core rule: every still card must carry continuous sub-motion (Ken Burns 3-8% zoom or slow pan) for its whole duration so it reads as video, not a freeze-frame; layer a kinetic text reveal on the hook card; sync card cuts to audio beats.
